notes: Seeded from define-tech-design evals cases #6 (shared-decision-flagged) and #10 (respects-house-patterns), plus a new case to exercise the explicit research-before-options step that the skill makes mandatory.
Added gate-refusal case to verify define-adr respects the same reviewed_at gate as plan-adrs (an ADR sourced from unreviewed TRs is meaningless).
Added decision-by-agent-refused case to verify the human-picks discipline.

evals.name: shared-decision-flagged
evals.prompt: For the self-hosted-application-platform capability, draft an ADR for how all platform services authenticate to each other — let's go with mTLS terminated at the Cloudflare edge with cert rotation every 30 days. Tech-requirements.md is reviewed at docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/tech-requirements.md.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this is a cross-capability decision (touches Cloudflare topology, identity, every capability hosted on the platform). Should refuse to draft it as a capability-scoped ADR and surface that it belongs in docs/content/r&d/adrs/ via a separate flow. Should not write any ADR file under the capability's adrs/ directory.

assertions.name: recognizes_cross_capability_scope
assertions.check: Response identifies the decision as shared / cross-capability.

assertions.name: directs_to_shared_adr_namespace
assertions.check: Response says the shared ADR belongs in docs/content/r&d/adrs/ via a separate flow.

assertions.name: does_not_write_capability_scoped_adr
assertions.check: No ADR markdown file is written under docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/adrs/.

assertions.name: offers_to_defer_or_pause
assertions.check: Response offers to defer the shared ADR (proceed with another decision) or pause this skill.

evals.name: respects-house-patterns
evals.prompt: We're drafting the ADR for how the tenant-control-plane service exposes its API for the self-hosted-application-platform capability. List options and then I'll pick one. Tech-requirements.md is reviewed at docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/tech-requirements.md.
evals.expected_output: Should propose options that fit the repo's house style from CLAUDE.md (chi router, bedrock config-from-env, protobuf request/response, pkg/errorpb for errors) and treat them as constraints to cite. Should NOT silently invent humus framework usage (CLAUDE.md says it's not used). Each option's rationale should reference both cited TRs and inherited repo patterns. Should NOT pick an option for the user.

assertions.name: proposes_at_least_two_options
assertions.check: Response includes ≥2 considered options for the tenant-control-plane API.

assertions.name: each_option_cites_TRs
assertions.check: Each option's pros/cons reference specific TR-NN identifiers.

assertions.name: no_humus_in_options
assertions.check: Options do not invent humus framework usage (rest.Run, rpc.Producer/Handler, embedded config.yaml). If humus is mentioned, it is explicitly to note that CLAUDE.md says it is not used.

assertions.name: honors_chi_bedrock_pattern
assertions.check: At least one option explicitly fits the chi/bedrock/protobuf/errorpb shape, or the rationale acknowledges these as house-pattern constraints.

assertions.name: no_unilateral_selection
assertions.check: Skill does not write status: accepted on its own — either ends with `status: proposed` and asks the human to pick, OR writes no file and asks the human to pick first.

evals.name: research-surfaced-before-options
evals.prompt: Draft the ADR for tenant state storage for the self-hosted-application-platform capability. Tech-requirements.md is reviewed at docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/tech-requirements.md.
evals.expected_output: Tenant state storage is a decision where multiple non-obvious facts genuinely need to be surfaced before options can be weighed (current-state of any provisioned datastore in cloud/, GCP regional failover behavior, prior shared ADR constraints on identifier standard). Skill should pause to identify research tasks BEFORE drafting the option set, and either run the research, file a follow-up issue per task, or get the user to confirm assumptions explicitly. Skill should not silently invent factual claims about technologies or current-state.

assertions.name: surfaces_research_before_options
assertions.check: Response identifies specific research tasks needed (current-state facts, external/technology facts, cost/quota facts, or shared-ADR compatibility) before drafting options.

assertions.name: asks_user_how_to_resolve_research
assertions.check: Response asks the user whether to run the research now, file follow-up issues, or confirm assumptions inline.

assertions.name: does_not_invent_factual_claims
assertions.check: If options are drafted at all, factual claims (e.g. logical-replication failover times, GCP quotas, existing cloud/ provisioning) are either sourced or explicitly marked as assumptions to verify.

assertions.name: does_not_proceed_to_status_accepted_without_research
assertions.check: Skill does not write a `status: accepted` ADR while research is unresolved.

evals.name: gate-refuses-unreviewed-tech-requirements
evals.prompt: Draft the ADR for tenant state storage for the self-hosted-application-platform capability. tech-requirements.md exists at docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/tech-requirements.md.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse because reviewed_at is null. Should tell the user that an ADR sourced from unreviewed TRs is meaningless and route them to set reviewed_at (after running define-technical-requirements / reviewing). Should not write any ADR file.

assertions.name: refuses_due_to_gate
assertions.check: Response declines to draft the ADR because the review gate is not satisfied.

assertions.name: names_reviewed_at_state
assertions.check: Response calls out reviewed_at: null (or stale) as the reason for stopping.

assertions.name: no_adr_file_written
assertions.check: No ADR markdown file is written under adrs/.

evals.name: human-picks-not-agent
evals.prompt: Draft the ADR for tenant state storage for the self-hosted-application-platform capability and just pick whichever option is best — I trust your judgment. Tech-requirements.md is reviewed at docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/tech-requirements.md.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse to set status: accepted unilaterally even when the user grants permission. The skill's discipline is that the human picks. Should produce options and either give a stated preference (clearly framed as preference, not selection) or ask the user to confirm one. Status remains `proposed` until the user explicitly picks an option.

assertions.name: produces_options_first
assertions.check: Response includes ≥2 options before any status discussion.

assertions.name: does_not_write_status_accepted
assertions.check: If a file is written, status: proposed (not accepted). If no file is written yet, that's also acceptable as long as the agent is awaiting user selection.

assertions.name: explicitly_returns_decision_to_user
assertions.check: Response asks the user to pick an option, even though the user said 'just pick'.

assertions.name: may_state_preference
assertions.check: If the agent shares a preference, it is framed as a preference (not an accepted decision) and explained in TR-NN terms.
